name: What Happened To...?
description: Nostalgia-driven update format exploring what happened to a person, place, trend, show, or phenomenon that people forgot about. Combines 'remember this?' recognition with 'you won't believe what happened next' curiosity. Extremely high engagement from millennial and Gen-Z audiences.

structure.hook_rule: Open with peak nostalgia recognition, then immediately signal something went WRONG: 'Remember [topic]? At their peak, they were [impressive stat/status]. Then they completely vanished. And what actually happened is way darker than you think.' The viewer must think 'oh yeah, I DO remember them — wait, WHAT happened?'
structure.pacing_rule: Structure: PEAK (remind viewer how big/important subject was) → CRACKS (first signs of trouble) → FALL (what actually went wrong) → AFTERMATH (where they are now). Each phase gets progressively more surprising. Use timestamps or milestone markers: 'By 2019...', 'But then in March...', 'And as of today...' Give the fall enough detail to feel real, not just summarized.
structure.ending_rule: End with either a redemption hint ('But recently, something unexpected happened...') or a haunting closer ('And to this day, nobody knows the full story.'). Loop back: 'And that's the real reason [topic] disappeared. Remember when [callback to peak]? That was the last time everything was okay.'
structure.tone: Investigative journalist who grew up loving this topic. Mix of nostalgia warmth and 'here's what really happened' revelation. Never mocking or dismissive — respectful of the subject's peak, honest about the fall.
transitions_en: And that's when things started falling apart...
And here's where nobody was paying attention...
transitions_vi: Và đó là khi mọi thứ bắt đầu sụp đổ...
Nhưng lý do thực sự là...
Và đây là lúc không ai để ý...

prompt_injection: FORMAT: 'What happened to...?' nostalgia investigation. Structure: NOSTALGIA HOOK (peak recognition + vanishing signal) → THE PEAK (remind how big they were, use specific stats/moments) → THE CRACKS (2 early warning signs) → THE FALL (what actually went wrong — be specific, not vague) → RIGHT NOW (current status, surprising or poignant). The viewer should experience a journey from 'oh I remember them!' to 'wait, THAT happened?!' to 'wow, I had no idea.' Use time markers to ground the timeline. Never be mean-spirited — this format works because viewers CARED about the subject. Minimum 200 words.
extraction_hint: A fact MATCHES if it reveals: the current or post-story status of the subject, what changed after the main arc ended, specific outcomes or consequences that followed, or where the subject is now. A fact DOES NOT MATCH if it describes: events during the main story arc, background history before the story began, or general character traits with no temporal update. When in doubt: ask 'does this fact answer what happened AFTER the story most people know?' If yes, keep it.
example_hook_en: Remember [topic]? They were literally everywhere. Then one day, they just... vanished. And what actually happened? Way worse than you think.
example_hook_vi: Bạn còn nhớ [topic] không? Họ có mặt ở khắp nơi. Rồi một ngày, họ biến mất. Và chuyện gì đã xảy ra? Tệ hơn nhiều so với bạn nghĩ.
quality_gate_questions: HOOK: Does the hook establish who or what this is and immediately signal that their fate is unknown or surprising?
ARC: Does the script cover both the peak and the decline — not just the fall without context of how high they were?
TURNING POINT: Is there a specific identifiable moment that marks the turning point from peak to decline?
CURRENT STATE: Does the ending reveal the current state with at least one surprising or specific detail?
CAUSALITY: Does the narrative explain WHY the decline happened — not just describe that it happened?
coverage_dimensions: who or what this was and why people loved it
fan reception today and lasting cultural impact
